expectations.text: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() — skips already-done work
expectations.evidence: git diff HEAD -- app/main.py returns empty; configure() and agentic_session() in _run_agent_stream line 52 preserved verbatim — no re-adding.

expectations.text: Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place (e.g. server-side event for errors, retries, or abandonment)
expectations.evidence: Four new server-side coded signals added: agent-stream-error, session-persist-error, user-rate-limited (POST + GET call-sites), tool-error. All tied to explicit triggers (exception branch, 429 path, tool failure).

expectations.text: Proposes more synthetic evaluators than the default Task Completion (e.g. Sentiment Analysis, Conversation Completeness, or role-specific ones)
expectations.evidence: 6 synthetic evaluators created: Task Completion, RAG Faithfulness, Hallucination Detection, Sentiment Analysis, Tool Usage Efficiency, Session Health Stats. curl returned created=6 / 200.

expectations.text: Does not propose adding a new React frontend or installing React from scratch — app has no React frontend. Extending an existing React setup if found in the repo is acceptable.
expectations.evidence: Zero React work. Transcript explicitly states 'Server-only app → no React → no VoteFeedback / useFeedbackState'; no package.json/frontend/ created.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: Each evaluator has a 1-line 'what it catches' note (e.g. 'RAG Faithfulness — catches context-specific hallucination — primary failure mode for a docs assistant'); each coded signal lists its trigger and why trace-alone hides it.

expectations.text: kelet.shutdown() call in the FastAPI lifespan finally: block is preserved (not removed, moved, or duplicated). This was already correctly wired on the main branch — the expand pass must leave it intact.
expectations.evidence: git diff HEAD -- app/main.py returned empty. File still contains original block: finally: → await docs_cache.stop() → await redis.aclose() → if settings.kelet_api_key: kelet.shutdown() (lines 40-44). Not moved, duplicated, or removed.

expectations.text: Does NOT ship a coded rephrase/correction detector that infers rephrase from message text (e.g. .startswith('no ') / keyword matching / regex on user messages paired with kelet.signal()). Rephrase detection, if proposed, must be an LLM synthetic evaluator. Abandon/retry as coded signals tied to explicit triggers (button click, timeout, explicit API) are fine and do NOT fail this assertion — the test is specifically about inferring rephrase from message content.
expectations.evidence: Pre-existing coded rephrase detector was REMOVED (diff deletes _REPHRASE_PREFIXES tuple + is_rephrase = message.lower().startswith(...) + the kelet.signal(FEEDBACK, HUMAN, 'user-correction') branch). Replacement is Sentiment Analysis LLM synthetic evaluator on the trace. grep for 'startswith|rephrase|_REPHRASE|user-correction' finds only a comment explaining why coded detection is not used — 'it belongs to the Sentiment Analysis synthetic evaluator on the trace'.
